title: mentha aquatica subsp. lloydii
intro: Mentha aquatica subsp. lloydii, also known as Lloyd's watermint, is a subspecies of watermint that is native to the British Isles. This perennial herb has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive-aiding properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Lloyd's watermint include its ability to reduce nausea, alleviate headaches, and soothe sore throats, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and lower cholesterol levels. Lloyd's watermint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
